title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Singles)
text:
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Singles) - SupportGoWhere
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Singles)
Share link
Provides up to $60,000 in Enhanced CPF Housing Grants (EHG) (Singles) for eligible first-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat on their own, or with their non-resident spouses.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
EHG (Singles) Grant of up to $60,000 for eligible first-timer applicants
Additional up to $60,000 grant for joint singles scheme (a total of up to $120,000)
Households with lower household incomes will receive a larger grant amount
Collapse all sections
About this support

The EHG (Singles) Grant provides up to $60,000 to offset the purchase price of the flat / reduce the required housing loan for the flat (new or resale)

Receive a total Grant of up to $120,000 when jointly purchasing flats with other eligible first-timer single applicants (Limited to 2, up to $60,000 each)

Who is this for?

First-timer applicants aged 35 or above, who are:

buying a HDB flat alone or with other first-timer single citizens

buying a resale flat alone, with other first-timer single citizens or with their parents

First-timer applicants aged 21 or above who are buying a resale flat with their non-resident spouse

If you are buying a flat with your parents, you and at least 1 parent must be a Singapore Citizen, and both parents must be first-timers and meet all the eligibility conditions

Applicants have worked continuously for at least 12 months, 2 months before the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application, and were employed at the time of the HFE letter application

Monthly household income not exceeding $4,500 for singles and $9,000 for joint singles, non-resident spouses or your parents (resale flats only)

Flat has a remaining lease of more than 20 years

To qualify for the full EHG grant, flat must have sufficient lease for the coverage of the youngest core applicant and occupier to the age of 95

Must not own, have an interest in or have disposed of any private residential property (local or overseas) within the last 30 months

All persons listed in the HFE letter application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property at HFE letter application and up to 30 months before HFE letter application

How to apply?

Obtain an HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal to determine your eligibility for this Grant as well as others

If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may apply for the grant when submitting your resale application or after you have booked a flat from HDB

The Grant will be credited to the CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible household members

Only core applicants can use their share of the housing grants to pay for the flat purchase

Where can I find help?

View more details

HDB Hotline: 6866-3066

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 22 May 2026
